title: The Whispering Well
summary: Ben moves to an old house with a mysterious well.
coverScene: An old stone well with a broken wooden cover sits in a dark, overgrown garden behind a quiet country house under the evening sky.

sentences.text: Ben had recently moved into a beautiful old house, which was situated near a quiet, dark forest far from the city.
Although the house itself was wonderful, he spent most of his time exploring the large, wild garden behind the property.
One afternoon, while he was clearing some thick plants, he discovered a deep stone well hidden under a wooden cover.
If the cover had not been so old, it would have prevented him from looking inside the dark hole.
He carefully removed the wet wood and looked down into the black water that lay silent at the very bottom.
A cold wind seemed to rise from the depth, which made him feel quite nervous but also extremely curious.
To test the depth of the old well, Ben decided to shout a simple greeting down into the darkness.
"Hello!" he cried, expecting to hear his own voice returned to him after a brief moment of silence.
Instead of a normal echo, a quiet whisper arose from the bottom, sounding like dry paper moving on the ground.
"I hear you," the voice said, which was clearly not the word that Ben had spoken into the well.
He stood completely still, wondering if his mind was playing a trick on him during this silent afternoon.
If he had been wiser, he would have covered the well immediately and walked back to his house.
The next morning, Ben was drawn back to the garden by an intense desire to hear the voice again.
He leaned over the stone edge, which was cold against his hands, and called out another question.
"Who is down there?" he asked, his heart beating fast as he waited for the strange response.
A soft sigh came from the black water, followed by a message that was whispered with a terrible tone.
"I am the one who waits in the dark, and I am getting warmer," the voice replied.
Although the message frightened him, he could not stop thinking about the unknown person who lived under the earth.
On the next day, Ben noticed a significant change in the sound of the whispers coming from the well.
When he shouted, "What do you want?" the reply did not seem to come from the very bottom anymore.
Instead, the words were whispered from a position that was several meters higher than the cold, dark water.
"I want to feel the sun, which I have not seen for a hundred years," the voice whispered back.
Ben took a step back, realizing that whatever was inside the well was actively climbing toward the light.
If he had possessed any sense of safety, he would have run away and never returned to that garden.
That evening, filled with fear, Ben decided that the well must be closed to protect himself from danger.
He gathered several heavy stones from around the yard and began to drop them onto the old wooden cover.
As each stone was placed, a low laugh was heard from the narrow gaps between the rough pieces of wood.
"Stones cannot hold me back, because I am already too close to the top," the voice whispered softly.
The sound was so near that Ben could almost feel the cold breath of the speaker on his face.
He dropped his last stone and ran inside his house, locking the door behind him with shaking hands.
That night, Ben could not sleep, as his mind was filled with images of the thing in the well.
He lay in his dark bedroom, listening to the wind that blew through the trees outside his window.
Suddenly, a sound arose that was not the wind; it was a familiar whisper coming from the floor.
"I am just outside your door, Ben," the voice said, sounding much clearer than it had before.
He covered his ears with his sheet, wishing that he had never bought this terrible, old country house.
If he had listened to the warnings of the neighbors, he would have moved to a different town.
When morning finally came, the bright sun provided some comfort, but Ben knew he was not safe.
He walked slowly into the garden to examine the well, expecting to find the heavy stones still in place.
To his shock, the stones had been pushed aside, and the wooden cover was completely broken into pieces.
A dark trail of wet ground led from the open hole toward the back door of his house.
It was clear that whatever had been living in the deep well was now walking on the grass.
If he had looked closer, he would have seen the wet marks that stopped right at his kitchen door.
Ben realized that he had to leave immediately, before the sun went down and darkness returned to the valley.
He ran to his bedroom, threw some clothes into a bag, and grabbed his car keys from the table.
As he was preparing to run out the front door, he heard a soft sound coming from the kitchen.
It was the slow, wet sound of something heavy dragging itself across the wooden floor toward the hall.
Then, he watched in fear as the handle of the kitchen door began to turn slowly.
A voice, which was now extremely close, whispered his name from the other side of the wood.
"Ben," the voice whispered, sounding like a wet throat trying to speak after a long period of silence.
"If you opened the door, we could finally look at each other in the beautiful light."
Ben did not wait to see what was behind the door; he jumped through the open living room window instead.
He ran to his car, started the engine, and drove away from the house without looking back even once.
Now, he lives in a busy city, but he always locks his doors and avoids any place with a well.
Sometimes, when it rains, he still hears that wet whisper coming from the pipes under his floor.

quiz.q: What did Ben find hidden under the wooden cover in his garden?
options: A hidden box of old letters.
A deep stone well.
A secret path to the forest.

quiz.q: How did the voice in the well change after the first few days?
options: It sounded closer to the top of the well.
It stopped replying to Ben's questions.
It became much louder and more angry.

quiz.q: What did Ben do when he heard the voice outside his kitchen door?
options: He opened the door to see who was there.
He ran to the well to throw more stones in.
He jumped through the living room window and drove away.

It was near a quiet, dark forest far from the city.

Instead, a quiet whisper came from the bottom.

If he is careful, he will cover the well and walk away.

He realized that the thing was climbing toward the light.
If he stays here, he will be in great danger.

He gathered heavy stones from around the yard.
He began to drop them onto the old wooden cover.
Then, he heard a low laugh from between the pieces of wood.

The sound was so near that he felt its cold breath.

He locked the door with shaking hands.
That night, Ben could not sleep at all.

Suddenly, he heard a familiar whisper from the floor.

It sounded much clearer than before.
He covered his ears with his sheet.

If he moves to another town, he will feel safe again.
In the morning, the bright sun made him feel a bit better.

He grabbed his car keys from the table.

It was a slow, wet sound from the kitchen.
He watched in fear as the door handle began to turn.
A voice whispered his name from the other side of the wood.

He jumped through the open living room window.
He ran to his car and drove away without looking back.
Now, he lives in a busy city.
He always locks his doors and avoids wells.

It comes from the ground under his floor.

quiz.q: What does Ben find in the garden behind his house?
options: A deep stone well.
A hidden wooden door.
A beautiful forest path.

options: To stop the cold wind from coming out.
To prevent the whispering thing from getting out.
To build a beautiful stone wall around it.

options: He runs through the front door.
He jumps through the kitchen window.
He jumps through the living room window.

sentences.text: Ben moves to a very old house.
It is far from the city.
He finds a well under a cover.
Ben takes the cover off.
He looks down into the black water.
He feels something bad.
But he wants to know more.
Ben calls into the well.
'Who is there?' he asks.
He wants his own sound back.
But a low sound comes up.
It says, 'I hear you.'
Ben does not say that.
He stands very still.
He should put the cover back.
Next morning, Ben goes back.
He asks another question.
'Who are you?' Ben asks.
A low sound comes from the water.
'I wait under you,' it says.
'I am getting close.'
Ben feels great fear.
But he can not stop.
The next day, the sound changes.
Ben asks, 'What do you want?'
The sound is much higher now.
It says, 'I want the light.'
'I do not see light for years.'
Ben steps back.
The thing is coming up.
Ben should run.
Before night, Ben feels fear.
He puts many things on the cover.
A low sound comes from it.
It says, 'You can not hold me.'
'I am near the top.'
The sound is very near.
Ben runs to his house.
He closes the door.
At night, Ben can not close his eyes.
He is in his black room.
He hears a low sound on his floor.
It says, 'I am outside your door.'
Ben covers his face.
He wants to leave now.
In the morning, there is light.
Ben goes outside to see the well.
The cover is in pieces.
He sees water on the ground.
The water goes to his door.
Something is out of the well.
It is now near his house.
Ben knows he must leave.
He runs to his room.
He takes his things and his car.
Then, he hears a sound.
Something is moving on the floor.
The door starts to turn.
A low sound speaks his name.
'Ben,' it says.
'Open the door.'
'We can look at each other.'
Ben does not open the door.
He goes out of the window.
He runs to his car.
He drives away.
Now, he lives in a city.
But he still hears that sound.
When there is water, he hears it.

options: A well under a cover.

options: He goes out of the window.
He goes out of the door.
